follow_up_questions: How many days ago did the fever start?
Is the fever constant or does it come and go?
Have you measured the temperature? What was it?
Do you have chills or shivering with the fever?
red_flags: fever with rash — consider dengue or chikungunya
fever with bleeding from any site — emergency, possible dengue hemorrhagic
fever with neck stiffness — consider meningitis
fever with altered consciousness — emergency
fever above 104F with no sweating — emergency
kerala_context: During monsoon: high risk of leptospirosis — ask about wading in floodwater
Year round: dengue is endemic — always ask about rash and joint pain with fever
Chikungunya common — ask about severe joint pain
Typhoid common — ask about relative bradycardia, rose spots, constipation
fever.clinical_notes: Always check for hemorrhagic fever signs in Kerala. Dengue NS1 antigen test recommended if fever >2 days with joint pain or rash.

follow_up_questions: Where exactly is the pain — centre, left, or right of chest?
Does the pain spread to your arm, jaw, or back?
Is it a crushing, squeezing, or sharp pain?
Do you get breathless with the pain?
red_flags: crushing central chest pain radiating to left arm or jaw — possible MI, EMERGENCY
chest pain with breathlessness and sweating — possible cardiac event
chest pain with collapse or syncope — EMERGENCY
sudden tearing chest pain radiating to back — possible aortic dissection
kerala_context: High prevalence of CAD in Kerala due to diet and lifestyle
Young patients with chest pain — consider myocarditis
Ask about family history of heart disease — very relevant in Kerala population
chest_pain.clinical_notes: Any chest pain with radiation or associated symptoms needs urgent ECG. Do not delay.

follow_up_questions: How long have you had the cough?
Are you bringing up any phlegm or sputum? What colour?
Is there any blood in the sputum?
Do you have fever along with the cough?
red_flags: cough with blood in sputum — rule out TB, lung cancer
cough with weight loss and night sweats — high suspicion TB
cough with severe breathlessness — possible pneumonia or COPD exacerbation
sudden onset cough with chest pain — consider pulmonary embolism
kerala_context: TB prevalence significant — always ask about night sweats and weight loss
High humidity increases fungal infections — ask about occupational exposure
Dust and pollution — ask about occupational history
cough.clinical_notes: Cough >2 weeks with fever and weight loss — mandatory sputum AFB. Chest X-ray for all productive coughs.

follow_up_questions: Where exactly is the headache — front, back, or one side?
Is it throbbing, pressure-like, or band-like?
Did it come on suddenly or gradually?
Do you have nausea or vomiting with the headache?
red_flags: thunderclap headache — worst headache of life, sudden onset — possible subarachnoid haemorrhage, EMERGENCY
headache with fever and neck stiffness — possible meningitis, EMERGENCY
headache with vision loss — possible raised ICP
headache after head injury — possible intracranial bleed
progressive worsening headache — rule out space-occupying lesion
kerala_context: Migraine very common — ask about triggers, aura, family history
Hypertension headache common — always check BP
Dengue headache — retro-orbital pain characteristic
headache.clinical_notes: Any new severe headache needs CT head urgently. Chronic migraine needs proper workup before treatment.

follow_up_questions: Where exactly is the pain — upper, lower, or all over the stomach?
Does the pain come in waves or is it constant?
Do you have vomiting or nausea?
Any changes in bowel habits — diarrhoea or constipation?
red_flags: severe sudden abdominal pain with rigid abdomen — possible perforation, EMERGENCY
abdominal pain with jaundice — liver or biliary pathology
abdominal pain with blood in stool — GI bleeding
right lower quadrant pain with fever — possible appendicitis
abdominal pain with inability to pass stool or gas — possible obstruction
kerala_context: Hepatitis A and E common — ask about jaundice, pale stools, dark urine
Amoebic dysentery common — ask about bloody diarrhoea
Food poisoning outbreaks common — ask about others affected
abdominal_pain.clinical_notes: Acute abdomen needs surgical review. Always ask about last menstrual period in females of reproductive age.

follow_up_questions: Which joints are affected?
Is there any swelling or redness over the joints?
Is the pain worse in the morning or evening?
Did the joint pain start with a fever?
red_flags: acute joint pain with fever and rash — dengue or chikungunya
single hot swollen joint with fever — septic arthritis, EMERGENCY
joint pain with chest pain and rash — possible SLE
joint pain after sore throat in child — possible rheumatic fever
kerala_context: Chikungunya endemic — severe migratory joint pain after fever
Rheumatoid arthritis common — morning stiffness >1 hour
Gout increasingly common — ask about diet, uric acid history
joint_pain.clinical_notes: Post-chikungunya arthritis can persist months. Always check for RF, ANA in chronic cases.

follow_up_questions: Did the breathlessness come on suddenly or gradually?
Does it happen at rest or only on exertion?
Do you wake up at night feeling breathless?
Do you have any wheezing or whistling sound when you breathe?
red_flags: sudden severe breathlessness at rest — EMERGENCY
breathlessness with chest pain — possible cardiac or PE
breathlessness with stridor — possible airway obstruction
breathlessness with cyanosis — EMERGENCY
kerala_context: Asthma very common — ask about triggers, previous attacks, inhalers
High humidity worsens COPD — ask about smoking history
Heart failure increasingly common — ask about leg swelling
breathlessness.clinical_notes: SpO2 mandatory for all breathless patients. Peak flow for asthma.

follow_up_questions: How long have you had diabetes?
What medicines are you taking for diabetes?
When did you last check your blood sugar? What was the reading?
Do you have any numbness or tingling in your feet?
red_flags: diabetic with fever — infection spreads fast, high priority
diabetic with chest pain — silent MI common in diabetics
diabetic with foot wound — diabetic foot, urgent review
diabetic with altered consciousness — hypoglycemia or DKA
kerala_context: Kerala has highest diabetes prevalence in India
Many patients on traditional medicine alongside allopathy — always ask
Diabetic nephropathy very common — check for proteinuria
diabetes.clinical_notes: Always check HbA1c, renal function, urine protein in diabetic patients. Foot examination mandatory.

follow_up_questions: Can you tell me more about your main complaint?
How long have you been feeling this way?
Has anything made it better or worse?
Do you have any other symptoms along with this?
red_flags: unexplained weight loss — rule out malignancy
night sweats with any complaint — rule out TB, lymphoma
unexplained fatigue — thyroid, anaemia, diabetes workup needed
kerala_context: Always ask about ayurvedic or herbal medicines
Nutritional deficiencies common despite food availability
Mental health concerns often presented as physical symptoms
general.clinical_notes: Complete systemic review important for vague complaints.
